items.title: Uber will bring its premium robotaxi service to Houston in 2027
items.summary: This will be the second market to have an Uber robotaxi service outfitted with Lucid EVs equipped with a self-driving system from Nuro.

items.title: Pinterest launches an experimental AI shopping app called ‘Ask Pinterest’
items.summary: Pinterest has launched 'Ask Pinterest,' an experimental AI-powered shopping app that lets users seek recommendations and inspiration through a conversational interface.

items.title: Anthropic’s latest feud with the Trump admin may actually help it, sales data suggests
items.summary: Anthropic's popularity with business users is growing so well that the latest beef with the government might actually boost it, data from Ramp suggests.

items.title: Apple plans to change its Hide My Email privacy feature that could make it less effective
items.summary: In the coming weeks, Apple will move anonymously generated emails addresses to a different domain.

items.title: SpaceX valuation balloons to $2.6T, briefly passes Amazon
items.summary: SpaceX's valuation has increased by $1 trillion since its shares started trading on Friday.

items.title: Qualcomm wants to be the chip inside whatever replaces your smartphone, and it just announced two products toward that end
items.summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon said Tuesday that the company is working on over 40 different AI wearable devices — including jewelry, earbuds with cameras, pins, and watches — a sign of how aggressively the chipmaker is betting that the next major computing platform won't be a phone.

items.title: Bug in FIFA World Cup internal system gave anyone ability to modify TV stream
items.summary: A security researcher said a flaw in FIFA’s online platforms allowed her to access several internal systems, including one that could have allowed her to take control of the TV stream of every World Cup match.

items.title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
items.summary: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7, introducing new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades. The launch is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google’s latest AI models to its devices.

items.title: Mobileye’s US robotaxi launch will put it on both sides of the AV business
items.summary: Mobileye apparently wants to own some of the robotaxi market, even if that puts it in direct competition with companies it supplies its self-driving system to.

items.title: Rivian cuts hundreds of workers after R2 deliveries start
items.summary: The company said the cuts were part of a restructuring meant to help scale to profitability. Rivian recently pushed back its profitability goal to invest in autonomy.

items.title: Snap finally debuts its long-awaited AR glasses, Specs, and, oof, they aren’t cheap
items.summary: For over a decade now, Snap has been working on this device. Now the glasses are finally here. So what stands out on first impression?

items.title: Sixty percent of US consumers say ‘AI’ in brand messaging is a turnoff, survey finds
items.summary: WordPress VIP’s latest survey suggests consumers are wary of AI-generated answers even as companies increasingly view AI search as an important referral channel.

items.title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
items.summary: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

items.title: India orders temporary ban on Telegram over exam fraud concerns
items.summary: The restrictions include a nationwide ban on Telegram until June 22 and a requirement to disable the app's message-editing feature.

items.title: Payments startup Flutterwave hits $3.2B valuation, backed by Ripple
items.summary: African payments infrastructure company Flutterwave has hit a new valuation and landed blockchain company Ripple as an investor and partner.

items.title: DOJ claims xAI’s unpermitted gas turbines are a matter of ‘national, economic, and energy security’
items.summary: The Justice department says the Pentagon needs xAI to keep using its unpermitted gas turbines.

items.title: Plaud says its software business topped $100M in ARR after shipping over 2M AI notetakers
items.summary: Plaud is trying to make a mark in a crowded market full of AI-powered meeting notetakers.

items.title: Robinhood’s note on 10% layoffs shows blaming AI isn’t cutting it
items.summary: Unlike many of his tech industry peers who have cut thousands of jobs citing the need to restructure  to make the most of AI, Robinhood's CEO Vlad Tenev conspicuously made no mention of AI in his note about layoffs.

items.title: Probably raises $9M to build a more reliable kind of AI
items.summary: Probably wants to prevent hallucinations and factual errors from reaching users, and achieve accuracy on par with deterministic systems.

items.title: This startup’s super metals could soon be in military drones, luxury watches, and chef’s knives
items.summary: Instead of heating metals, Foundation Alloy beats them into submission. The startup has raised $22 million to scale up production of its alloys.
